channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: TOCA Online Virtual Language Fellowship: Study Arabic, French, or Spanish with Global Peers Through Cultural Exchange
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Language learning is no longer limited to classrooms or textbooks. With the increasing importance of global communication and cultural understanding, innovative programs are emerging to connect students worldwide. One such initiative is TOCA Online, a virtual language learning and cultural exchange program that provides students with the opportunity to build language skills while connecting with […]

The post TOCA Online Virtual Language Fellowship: Study Arabic, French, or Spanish with Global Peers Through Cultural Exchange appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Language learning is no longer limited to classrooms or textbooks. With the increasing importance of global communication and cultural understanding, innovative programs are emerging to connect students worldwide. One such initiative is TOCA Online, a virtual language learning and cultural exchange program that provides students with the opportunity to build language skills while connecting with diverse communities across the globe.

Implemented by Teens of Color Abroad (TOCA) and supported by the J. Christopher Stevens Virtual Exchange Initiative (JCSVEI) under the U.S. Department of State’s Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs, TOCA Online aims to create meaningful international connections through virtual exchange.

The program is designed for high school and university students who want to improve their language proficiency, develop intercultural communication skills, and engage in global conversations with peers and refugee educators worldwide.

What is TOCA Online?

TOCA Online is a virtual language and cultural exchange program that allows students to learn languages such as Arabic, French, and Spanish from native speakers. The program places a strong emphasis on dialogue, cultural awareness, and global collaboration.

Students are taught by refugee language partners who are native speakers of the language they teach. These instructors are located in different parts of the world and bring valuable cultural perspectives into each lesson. Through this approach, students gain both linguistic competence and cultural understanding.

The program also integrates workshops, peer exchanges, and collaborative projects designed to encourage cross-cultural dialogue and global awareness among participants.

Program Structure

TOCA Online offers two main program tracks:

High School Virtual Language Program

The high school program is designed for students between the ages of 13 and 18 and runs for three weeks. During this period, students participate in virtual language instruction sessions combined with cultural exchange activities.

Participants can choose from several language options depending on their interests and goals.

Available courses include:

  • Introduction to Arabic (20 hours)

  • Introduction to French (10 hours)

  • Introduction to Spanish (10 hours)

  • Advanced Spanish (10 hours)

Students take part in live online sessions where they practice speaking and listening skills while interacting with peers from around the world.

In addition to language classes, students attend Refugee Voices workshops, which feature distinguished refugee speakers who share their stories and experiences. These sessions provide students with insights into global migration, resilience, and cultural diversity.

College-Level Arabic Language Exchange Program

TOCA Online also offers a college-level program focused on Arabic language practice and cross-cultural collaboration.

This program connects students from Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) in the United States with university students from the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.

Participants engage in:

  • One-on-one Arabic and English language exchanges

  • Interactive online modules

  • Cultural workshops

  • Collaborative virtual sessions

The exchange typically runs for seven to eight weeks, allowing participants to build meaningful connections while improving their language skills.

At the end of the program, American participants submit short video introductions in Arabic demonstrating their newly acquired skills, while MENA participants present their experiences collaborating with U.S. students and discuss how the program helped prepare them for the workforce.

Currently, the college-level opportunity is available only to students from partnering HBCUs.

Program Options and Costs

TOCA Online provides two participation options for high school students.

Standard Program

The standard program is offered free of charge, making it accessible to students from various backgrounds.

Participants in the standard program receive:

  • Live virtual language instruction

  • Refugee Voices workshops

  • Cultural exchange sessions

  • A digital certificate of completion

  • Access to alumni opportunities and networking resources

Immersive Program

Students seeking a more intensive experience can enroll in the Immersive Program, which costs USD 250.

This option includes all benefits of the standard program plus additional features such as:

  • Five hours of personalized one-on-one language practice

  • A TOCA completion package containing a t-shirt, pen, sticker, printed certificate, and activity book

  • Invitations to three virtual culinary lessons

The immersive option allows participants to deepen their language practice and gain a richer cultural experience.

Key Benefits of Participating in TOCA Online

Participating in TOCA Online offers several valuable benefits for students interested in international education and cross-cultural communication.

Development of Language Skills

Students significantly improve their speaking and listening skills by interacting directly with native speakers through live sessions.

Cultural Exchange and Global Awareness

The program fosters meaningful cultural exchange by connecting students with peers and educators from diverse backgrounds worldwide.

Networking and Global Connections

Participants build friendships and professional connections with other globally minded students and educators.

Access to Future Opportunities

Students receive access to scholarships, educational opportunities, and international programs through TOCA’s alumni network and newsletters.

Certificates and Recommendations

Participants earn a certificate of completion and may qualify for letters of recommendation to support future academic and career pursuits.

Why Virtual Exchange Matters

Virtual exchange programs like TOCA Online are becoming increasingly important in the modern educational landscape. They allow students to connect with people across borders, develop intercultural competence, and gain real-world communication skills.

These experiences prepare young people for global careers in fields such as international relations, diplomacy, global business, education, and humanitarian work.

By engaging with students and educators worldwide, participants develop empathy, cultural understanding, and the ability to collaborate across cultural and geographic boundaries.

Testimonials from Participants

Students, educators, and parents consistently praise the impact of the TOCA Online program.

One educator noted that the program complements classroom learning by reinforcing language skills in an interactive environment.

Parents have also highlighted the program’s impact on their children’s academic and career paths. Some participants have gone on to pursue language studies at the university level after completing the program.

Many students report gaining confidence in speaking their target language and developing meaningful international friendships through the program.

Application Timeline

The application cycle for the 2025 TOCA Online program has closed. Students interested in participating in the future are encouraged to join the program’s newsletter to receive updates when applications open for the 2026 season.

Applicants may be invited to participate in a virtual interview with TOCA staff to discuss their interest and commitment to the program.

Prospective participants are encouraged to monitor announcements to ensure they do not miss the next application cycle.

for more information click here

Discover more opportunities here  

The post TOCA Online Virtual Language Fellowship: Study Arabic, French, or Spanish with Global Peers Through Cultural Exchange appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Government Scholarship for International Students to Study in New Zealand
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships programme is one of the most prestigious international education opportunities funded by the Government of New Zealand. Designed to support sustainable development and strengthen global partnerships, the scholarship offers fully funded opportunities for students from eligible developing countries to pursue higher education in New Zealand. Administered through the New Zealand […]

The post Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Government Scholarship for International Students to Study in New Zealand appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships programme is one of the most prestigious international education opportunities funded by the Government of New Zealand. Designed to support sustainable development and strengthen global partnerships, the scholarship offers fully funded opportunities for students from eligible developing countries to pursue higher education in New Zealand.

Administered through the New Zealand Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade under the New Zealand Aid Programme and supported by institutions such as Education New Zealand and Massey University, the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship aims to empower talented individuals with the knowledge, leadership skills, and professional expertise needed to contribute to the social and economic development of their home countries.

This scholarship is highly competitive and provides comprehensive funding for international students to pursue undergraduate and postgraduate studies in New Zealand universities and institutions.

Purpose of the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships

The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships were created to strengthen international relationships and support global development by investing in education. The scholarship programme focuses on building leadership capacity among students from developing countries.

Through this initiative, scholars gain world-class education in New Zealand while developing skills and expertise that can contribute to the long-term development of their home nations.

The programme aims to:

  • Improve professional and technical skills among international scholars

  • Promote sustainable development in partner countries

  • Strengthen diplomatic and educational ties between New Zealand and developing nations

  • Provide students with the knowledge required to address economic, social, and environmental challenges in their countries

Scholars are expected to return home after completing their studies to apply their knowledge and contribute to their nation’s development.

Scholarship Benefits and Funding Coverage

The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity, meaning selected scholars receive comprehensive financial support for the duration of their studies.

Benefits may include:

1. Full Tuition Fees – The scholarship covers the full cost of tuition at approved universities or institutes in New Zealand.

2. Living Allowance – Scholars receive a regular stipend to cover daily living expenses such as accommodation, food, and personal costs.

3. International Travel – The scholarship covers round-trip airfare from the scholar’s home country to New Zealand at the start and end of the scholarship.

4. Travel Insurance – Recipients are provided with medical and travel insurance during their scholarship period.

5. Home Leave Travel – Students studying longer than 1.6 years may be eligible for one or two trips home during the scholarship period, depending on the program duration and border regulations.

6. Research and Thesis Support – Postgraduate scholars may receive financial support for thesis preparation and research-related costs.

7. Tutoring Assistance – Certain undergraduate programmes may receive additional tutoring support of up to NZ$1000 per year.

8. Reintegration Allowance – After completing the scholarship, scholars receive a NZ$1000 reintegration allowance to help them settle back into their home country.

Eligible Fields of Study

The scholarship supports a wide range of academic disciplines. However, the New Zealand Aid Programme prioritizes fields that directly support the development needs of participating countries.

Common priority areas include:

  • Agriculture and environmental science

  • Climate change and sustainability

  • Engineering and infrastructure

  • Public health and medical sciences

  • Education and teacher training

  • Business and economic development

  • Governance and public policy

  • Disaster risk management

Applicants are encouraged to select courses aligned with the development priorities of their home countries.

Category of People Eligible to Apply

The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship targets individuals who demonstrate leadership potential and commitment to contributing to the development of their home country.

Eligible categories include:

  • Undergraduate students seeking a bachelor’s degree

  • Graduates applying for master’s or postgraduate degrees

  • Professionals seeking advanced academic training

  • Government officials or development professionals

  • Community leaders and researchers

Applicants must show a strong commitment to returning to their country after completing their studies.

Countries and Regions Eligible for the Scholarship

The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship is available to citizens of specific eligible countries, mainly from the Pacific and parts of Asia.

Eligible regions typically include:

Pacific Countries

Examples include:

  • Fiji

  • Samoa

  • Tonga

  • Solomon Islands

  • Kiribati

  • Tuvalu

  • Vanuatu

  • Niue

ASEAN and Selected Asian Countries

Certain countries in Southeast and South Asia may also qualify.

Applicants must confirm that their country is listed among the official eligible countries before applying.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet a number of eligibility criteria before applying for the scholarship:

1. Age Requirement – Applicants must be at least 18 years old when the scholarship begins.

2. Citizenship – Applicants must hold citizenship from one of the eligible countries listed in the scholarship programme.

3. Residency Requirement – Applicants are generally required to have lived in their home country for a certain period before applying.

4. Academic Requirements – Applicants must meet admission requirements for their chosen university and programme in New Zealand.

5. Commitment to Home Country Development – Applicants must demonstrate their willingness to return home after completing their studies and contribute to national development.

6. Work Experience – Some programmes, particularly postgraduate ones, may require relevant professional experience.

7. Scholarship History – Applicants who have previously received a Manaaki scholarship may not be eligible to apply again.

Study Mode and Location

Scholarship recipients must study full-time and on campus in New Zealand.

Students may enroll in one of New Zealand’s universities or institutes of technology offering eligible programmes.

Application Process

The application process for the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship involves several stages.

Step 1: Check Eligibility

Applicants must complete the scholarship eligibility questionnaire to determine whether they qualify for the programme.

Step 2: Research Courses

Applicants should identify suitable courses offered by New Zealand institutions that align with their academic background and career goals.

Step 3: Register Online

Eligible applicants must create an account on the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship online portal.

Step 4: Submit Application

Applicants must complete and submit their application before the deadline.

Step 5: Selection Process

Applications are assessed based on:

  • Academic merit

  • Leadership potential

  • Development impact

  • Commitment to contributing to the applicant’s home country

Apply here

Selection Criteria

The selection committee considers several factors when evaluating applications, including:

  • Academic excellence

  • Professional achievements

  • Leadership potential

  • Development impact of proposed study

  • Alignment with national development priorities

Applicants who perform well during the evaluation process may be shortlisted and invited for interviews.

Important Deadline

Applicants must submit their application before the official deadline.

Application Deadline: 31st March 2026

Late applications are not accepted, and the application portal may close early if the maximum number of submissions is reached.

Why the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship Is Highly Competitive

The Manaaki scholarship is considered one of the most prestigious international scholarships for students from developing nations. Its comprehensive funding package, high-quality education opportunities, and focus on leadership development make it extremely competitive.

Thousands of applicants from around the world compete each year for a limited number of scholarships.

Successful candidates usually demonstrate:

  • Strong academic records

  • Leadership potential

  • Clear development goals

  • Commitment to returning home after graduation

The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships represent a life-changing opportunity for students from eligible developing countries who wish to pursue world-class education in New Zealand. By providing full financial support and access to top universities, the programme empowers scholars to gain advanced knowledge, develop leadership skills, and contribute meaningfully to the growth of their nations. For individuals who aspire to study abroad while making a lasting impact in their communities, the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship is an opportunity worth pursuing.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Government Scholarship for International Students to Study in New Zealand appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Institute for Youth in Policy Public Policy Fellowship 2026: Global Opportunity for Young Leaders to Develop Real-World Policy Solutions
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Institute for Youth in Policy (YIP) Public Policy Fellowship is a highly competitive international leadership and policy development program designed for emerging young leaders interested in shaping the future of public policy. This intensive two-month fellowship empowers participants to collaborate, conduct policy analysis, and propose innovative cross-sector solutions addressing critical global challenges in social, […]

The post Institute for Youth in Policy Public Policy Fellowship 2026: Global Opportunity for Young Leaders to Develop Real-World Policy Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Institute for Youth in Policy (YIP) Public Policy Fellowship is a highly competitive international leadership and policy development program designed for emerging young leaders interested in shaping the future of public policy. This intensive two-month fellowship empowers participants to collaborate, conduct policy analysis, and propose innovative cross-sector solutions addressing critical global challenges in social, political, economic, and environmental systems.

With an acceptance rate averaging 8–10%, the fellowship is one of the most selective youth policy development opportunities available. It brings together talented students, researchers, activists, and future policymakers from around the world to learn directly from experienced mentors, policy professionals, and academic leaders.

Participants gain hands-on experience in policy research, project management, communication, and teamwork while developing a comprehensive capstone project designed to influence real-world policy discussions.

About the Institute for Youth in Policy Fellowship

The Public Policy Fellowship is a flagship program of the Institute for Youth in Policy that focuses on cultivating the next generation of policy thinkers and civic leaders. The fellowship is built around collaborative learning, mentorship, and practical policy development.

The program lasts two months and provides a structured environment where fellows examine public policy through local, national, and global perspectives. Fellows work closely with peers and policy professionals to evaluate current policy issues and design actionable solutions.

The fellowship emphasizes interdisciplinary thinking and encourages participants to explore diverse sectors such as:

  • Social policy and civic engagement

  • Economic development and governance

  • Environmental sustainability

  • Urban development and renewal

  • Technology policy and smart infrastructure

  • Education reform

  • Public sector innovation

By the end of the program, fellows will have produced a policy-focused capstone project and gained practical skills that help them engage meaningfully with public policy processes.

Fellowship Impact and Achievements

The Institute for Youth in Policy fellowship has already demonstrated significant global impact through the work of its participants.

Program achievements include:

  • 1000+ fellows have graduated from the fellowship program

  • 300+ policy capstone projects have been published

  • 3.2 million views recorded on fellows’ published policy capstones

  • 80% of YIP leadership members originally participated in the fellowship

  • 8–10% acceptance rate, highlighting the program’s competitive selection process

These numbers demonstrate the program’s growing reputation as a leading youth policy initiative.

Fellowship Program Structure

The fellowship is structured to combine education, mentorship, collaboration, and practical policy development.

During the two-month program, fellows will participate in:

1. Policy Workshops and Learning Sessions – Participants attend workshops led by policy experts, academics, and experienced practitioners. These sessions introduce fellows to real policy frameworks and analytical tools used in policymaking.

2. Collaborative Research Projects – Fellows work in teams with peers from diverse backgrounds to research and develop policy ideas. Collaboration is a core element of the program, allowing fellows to explore different perspectives.

3. Mentorship and Networking – Participants engage with YIP department executives, policy experts, and civic leaders who provide guidance throughout the program.

4. Policy Analysis and Project Development – Fellows conduct research on key issues and develop actionable policy recommendations.

5. Capstone Project – Each fellow completes a policy capstone project, which is published and shared with the wider policy community.

Capstone projects often focus on:

  • Urban policy and city development

  • Climate and environmental policy

  • Education systems reform

  • Digital governance and technology

  • Social equity initiatives

  • Economic policy innovation

These projects represent the culmination of the fellow’s research and collaboration throughout the program.

What Fellows Gain from the Program

The fellowship is designed to provide both academic insight and practical policy experience.

Participants benefit from:

1. Policy Development Skills – Fellows learn how policies are designed, analyzed, and implemented in real-world governance structures.

2. Leadership Development – Participants build leadership abilities through teamwork, collaborative decision-making, and project management.

3. Research and Analytical Skills – The fellowship teaches structured approaches to policy analysis and data-driven problem solving.

4. Communication and Advocacy Skills – Fellows develop the ability to communicate policy ideas clearly through writing, presentations, and public engagement.

5. Professional Networking – Participants build connections with policymakers, educators, and civic leaders that can support future careers.

Fellowship Cohort Dates for 2026

The Institute for Youth in Policy offers multiple fellowship sessions each year.

Winter Cohort

17th January 2026 – 21st March 2026

Spring Cohort

11th April 2026 – 6th June 2026

Summer Cohort

20th June 2026 – 22nd August 2026

Fall Cohort

12th September 2026 – 7th November 2026

Applicants should apply before the relevant cohort deadline for the Spring 2026 cohort and decisions will be announced on 1st April 2026.

Geographic Eligibility

The Institute for Youth in Policy fellowship is open to applicants from around the world.

Eligible applicants may come from:

  • Africa

  • Asia

  • Europe

  • North America

  • South America

  • Oceania

This global participation helps ensure diverse perspectives and collaborative policy discussions.

Qualifications and Requirements

Applicants are typically expected to demonstrate:

  • A strong interest in public policy or governance

  • Motivation to create social, economic, environmental, or political change

  • Ability to collaborate with international peers

  • Strong communication and critical thinking skills

  • Commitment to completing the full two-month fellowship

Although specific academic requirements are not strictly limited, applicants with demonstrated leadership, academic curiosity, and civic engagement experience are especially encouraged to apply.

What Happens After the Fellowship

Graduates of the fellowship often continue their policy work in various ways.

Many fellows go on to:

  • Join leadership roles within the Institute for Youth in Policy

  • Publish policy research and commentary

  • Participate in civic initiatives and policy advocacy

  • Pursue careers in government, law, international development, or public service

  • Launch policy initiatives within their communities

Because fellows publish their capstone projects, participants leave the program with tangible work that can strengthen academic or professional portfolios.

Who Can Apply

The fellowship is designed for young emerging leaders who are passionate about public policy and civic engagement.

Eligible Categories of Applicants

The program welcomes applications from individuals such as:

  • High school students

  • University students

  • Recent graduates

  • Youth activists

  • Aspiring policymakers

  • Researchers interested in governance

  • Civic engagement advocates

Applicants should demonstrate a strong interest in social impact and public policy.

How to Apply

Interested applicants must submit their applications before the relevant deadline through the official application form on the Institute for Youth in Policy website.

Applicants should prepare to provide information about their interests in public policy, leadership experiences, and motivations for joining the fellowship.

Due to the highly selective nature of the program, applicants are encouraged to submit thoughtful and well-prepared applications.

Apply here

Application Deadline: 27th March 2026 (Spring Cohort)

The Institute for Youth in Policy fellowship represents a powerful platform for youth engagement in policymaking. In a world facing complex global challenges, the program provides young leaders with the knowledge, tools, and networks needed to develop innovative solutions. By fostering collaboration between students, policymakers, and civic leaders, the fellowship helps ensure that the voices of young people are represented in shaping future policy decisions.

For more information click here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Institute for Youth in Policy Public Policy Fellowship 2026: Global Opportunity for Young Leaders to Develop Real-World Policy Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WWF BRIDGE Paid Summer Internship 2026: Fully Paid Global Internship Opportunity for Students Interested in Conservation Careers
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Students and young professionals seeking a meaningful career in environmental protection and sustainability now have a major opportunity through the World Wildlife Fund (WWF). The WWF BRIDGE Internship Program offers a highly competitive paid summer internship designed to introduce undergraduate and graduate students to the conservation sector while providing practical professional experience. The BRIDGE program […]

The post WWF BRIDGE Paid Summer Internship 2026: Fully Paid Global Internship Opportunity for Students Interested in Conservation Careers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students and young professionals seeking a meaningful career in environmental protection and sustainability now have a major opportunity through the World Wildlife Fund (WWF). The WWF BRIDGE Internship Program offers a highly competitive paid summer internship designed to introduce undergraduate and graduate students to the conservation sector while providing practical professional experience.

The BRIDGE program has become one of the most recognized conservation internships globally, giving students exposure to real-world environmental work, mentorship from leading experts, and training designed to prepare them for careers in sustainability, climate action, wildlife protection, and environmental policy.

About the WWF BRIDGE Internship Program

The WWF BRIDGE Internship Program is a paid internship initiative launched in 2021 to support students interested in careers related to environmental conservation, sustainability, biodiversity protection, and climate change solutions. The program was designed to create pathways for talented students who may not previously have had professional exposure to conservation careers.

The goal of the program is to connect the next generation of leaders with opportunities that combine professional development, mentorship, and hands-on project work within WWF’s global conservation initiatives.

The BRIDGE Internship seeks to:

  • Increase exposure of students to careers in conservation and sustainability

  • Provide practical work experience in the environmental sector

  • Reduce barriers that prevent students from accessing professional internships

  • Create a pipeline of future conservation professionals and leaders

Through this initiative, interns work directly with WWF departments on research, communications, policy, climate programs, environmental strategy, and more.

Since its launch, the program has attracted thousands of applicants and has produced hundreds of alumni who are now working in environmental careers across the globe.

Internship Benefits and Salary

One of the major advantages of the WWF BRIDGE Internship is that it is fully paid, ensuring that students can participate without financial barriers.

Internship Pay

  • Undergraduate interns: $20.50 per hour

  • Graduate interns: $22.50 per hour

The internship typically runs 35 hours per week for approximately 10 weeks.

Additional Benefits

Participants in the BRIDGE Internship also gain access to several professional and workplace benefits, including:

  • Professional training programs

  • Resume writing and interview preparation workshops

  • Salary negotiation training

  • Networking opportunities with WWF leadership

  • Access to the eCornell professional development program

  • Exposure to WWF global conservation projects

  • Mentorship and career guidance

  • Flexible work arrangements including virtual and hybrid positions

Interns also benefit from workplace amenities such as:

  • Compressed work schedules with every other Friday off

  • Paid sick leave for certain locations

  • Access to employee assistance resources

    • Forest conservation research

    • Biodiversity protection

    • Climate change mitigation projects

    • Natural resource management

    • Marine conservation

      Professional networking events with WWF staff and experts.

Fields and Internship Areas

Interns in the BRIDGE program can work across various WWF departments depending on available positions.

Examples of previous internship roles include:

Conservation and Environmental Programs

  • Forest conservation research

  • Biodiversity protection

  • Climate change mitigation projects

  • Natural resource management

  • Marine conservation

Research and Policy

  • Environmental policy research

  • Sustainable development strategies

  • Climate risk analysis

  • Impact investing and sustainability markets

Communications and Marketing

  • Environmental awareness campaigns

  • Social media strategies for conservation

  • Public outreach initiatives

Development and Partnerships

  • Grant proposal research

  • Environmental funding initiatives

  • Corporate sustainability partnerships

Program Operations

  • Sustainable procurement strategies

  • Institutional sustainability planning

These roles allow interns to gain direct experience working on global environmental challenges.

Countries and Regions Eligible

The WWF BRIDGE Internship is open internationally, meaning students from all countries and continents may apply depending on the specific internship role and location.

Eligible applicants can come from regions including:

  • Africa

  • Asia

  • Europe

  • North America

  • South America

  • Oceania

However, some internship roles may have specific location or work authorization requirements, particularly for positions based in the United States or hybrid roles.

Required Qualifications

While the program welcomes applicants from diverse academic backgrounds, strong candidates usually demonstrate:

Academic Qualifications

Applicants should be studying fields such as:

  • Environmental science

  • Wildlife conservation

  • Ecology

  • Sustainability

  • Climate science

  • International development

  • Environmental policy

  • Biology

  • Natural resource management

  • Communications or marketing (for outreach roles)

  • Business or finance (for sustainability investment roles)

Skills and Competencies

Applicants are expected to demonstrate:

  • Strong interest in environmental conservation

  • Passion for sustainability and climate action

  • Research and analytical skills

  • Teamwork and collaboration abilities

  • Communication skills

  • Organizational and time management skills

Graduate-level internships may require strong research skills and analytical capabilities.

Internship Structure

The WWF BRIDGE Internship lasts 10 weeks during the summer, typically running from June to August.

Work Format

Internships may be offered in different formats:

  • Virtual internships

  • Hybrid internships

  • On-site placements

This flexible structure allows students from different regions to participate.

Workload

  • Approximately 35 hours per week

  • Full-time summer commitment

Interns collaborate with WWF teams on real-world projects that contribute directly to the organization’s mission.

Professional Development Opportunities

One of the most valuable aspects of the BRIDGE Internship is the extensive professional development offered to participants.

These opportunities include:

  • Leadership training

  • Career mentoring

  • Resume building workshops

  • Interview preparation sessions

  • Networking sessions with WWF leadership

  • Professional certification programs

Participants also gain access to the Cornell University eCornell training program, which offers online professional education courses.

These development activities help interns build a strong foundation for careers in conservation, sustainability, and environmental policy.

Program Impact

Since its launch, the BRIDGE Internship has become one of the most impactful programs for early-career conservationists.

Key achievements include:

  • Thousands of applicants from around the world

  • Over 180 alumni working in conservation-related careers

  • Many interns later returning to work at WWF or other environmental organizations.

The program plays a critical role in helping students from diverse backgrounds enter the conservation sector.

Who Can Apply

The WWF BRIDGE Internship is designed primarily for students and early-career professionals who are interested in environmental conservation and sustainability.

Eligible Applicants

The program accepts applicants who are currently enrolled in:

  • Associate degree programs

  • Bachelor’s degree programs

  • Master’s degree programs

  • Doctoral programs (with limited coursework completed)

Applicants must be active students at the time of application.

Doctoral Student Requirement

Doctoral students must not have completed more than two years of coursework to qualify for the internship.

Application Requirements

Applicants must prepare the following documents:

Required Documents

  1. Resume or Curriculum Vitae (CV)

  2. Cover Letter explaining interest in the internship

  3. Letter of recommendation (requested for final candidates)

The cover letter should clearly demonstrate the applicant’s passion for conservation, sustainability, and environmental impact.

How to Apply

To apply for the WWF BRIDGE Internship:

  1. Visit the official WWF careers website.

  2. Search for available BRIDGE internship positions.

  3. Select the internship that matches your skills and interests.

  4. Submit your application with the required documents.

Available positions are usually posted on the WWF careers page starting in late March each year.

Apply here

Application Deadline

Applications typically open in late March, with many listings closing around May 2026 depending on the specific internship role.

Why You Should Apply

The WWF BRIDGE Internship is one of the most valuable opportunities for students who want to build a career in environmental sustainability.

Key reasons to apply include:

  • Paid internship with competitive hourly wages

  • Hands-on conservation experience

  • Networking with global environmental leaders

  • Professional training and career development

  • Exposure to real-world environmental challenges

  • Opportunity to work with one of the most respected conservation organizations in the world

For students passionate about protecting biodiversity, fighting climate change, and shaping sustainable policies, the BRIDGE Internship provides a powerful entry point into the environmental sector.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post WWF BRIDGE Paid Summer Internship 2026: Fully Paid Global Internship Opportunity for Students Interested in Conservation Careers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FAO Fellows Programme 2026: Join the United Nations to Advance Global Food Security and Sustainable Agriculture
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) has opened applications for the FAO Fellows Programme 2026, a prestigious international opportunity designed for PhD students, researchers, and professors who want to contribute to global agricultural development, food security, and sustainable agri-food systems. This fellowship program offers highly qualified individuals the opportunity to work […]

The post FAO Fellows Programme 2026: Join the United Nations to Advance Global Food Security and Sustainable Agriculture appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) has opened applications for the FAO Fellows Programme 2026, a prestigious international opportunity designed for PhD students, researchers, and professors who want to contribute to global agricultural development, food security, and sustainable agri-food systems.

This fellowship program offers highly qualified individuals the opportunity to work alongside experts at one of the world’s most influential international organizations addressing hunger, food systems transformation, and sustainable agricultural development.

Participants in the FAO Fellows Programme will gain valuable experience supporting FAO initiatives, contributing technical knowledge to global development programs, and engaging in research and policy work aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

The fellowship takes place across FAO’s global network, including Regional Offices, Sub-regional Offices, Country Offices, Liaison Offices, and FAO Headquarters.

About the FAO Fellows Programme

The FAO Fellows Programme is designed to attract experienced researchers and professionals who possess advanced academic expertise and a strong commitment to global development.

Through this program, fellows contribute their technical knowledge to ongoing FAO initiatives while developing specialized learning objectives aligned with the organization’s mission.

The program is particularly focused on strengthening agri-food systems, food security, sustainable agriculture, and rural development worldwide.

Fellows collaborate with FAO teams to support policy research, technical programs, and analytical activities that help address some of the world’s most pressing development challenges.

Programme Location

Selected fellows may be placed in different FAO offices worldwide, including:

  • FAO Headquarters

  • Regional Offices

  • Sub-regional Offices

  • Country Offices

  • Liaison Offices

This global placement structure allows fellows to gain international exposure and contribute to diverse development contexts.

Programme Duration

The FAO Fellows Programme lasts up to 11 months.

During this period, fellows work closely with FAO specialists and program teams while contributing to research, policy analysis, and program implementation.

Key Responsibilities

Participants in the FAO Fellows Programme will engage in a wide range of activities designed to support the organization’s mission and projects.

Typical responsibilities include:

Supporting technical and research work within FAO programmes.

Providing specialized expertise in areas related to agriculture, food systems, rural development, climate resilience, or international development.

Contributing to research activities, policy analysis, and program implementation.

Assisting FAO teams with analytical work and technical reporting.

Participating in program planning, monitoring, and evaluation activities.

Working under the supervision of an assigned FAO specialist or program manager.

Minimum Requirements

To qualify for the FAO Fellows Programme 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Applicants must hold a Master’s or PhD degree, be currently enrolled in a PhD programme, or be a recent graduate with advanced academic training.

Candidates must demonstrate at least two years of relevant work experience or research experience in a field related to agriculture, food security, environmental sustainability, or development studies.

Applicants must possess working knowledge of at least one FAO official language, which includes:

Arabic
Chinese
English
French
Russian
Spanish

Candidates must be nationals of an FAO Member country.

Strong communication skills and the ability to work effectively in multicultural and international environments are required.

Important Information

It is important for applicants to note several key conditions associated with the FAO Fellows Programme.

The fellowship is classified as a non-staff opportunity, meaning fellows are not considered FAO employees.

FAO does not provide remuneration for fellows participating in the program. Therefore, fellows must secure sponsorship or financial support from an external institution, such as a university, research institute, or government body.

Selected candidates will undergo reference verification and background checks before final confirmation.

FAO strongly encourages applications from women, individuals from underrepresented nationalities, and persons with disabilities, in line with the organization’s commitment to diversity and inclusion.

Why You Should Apply for the FAO Fellows Programme

The FAO Fellows Programme provides a unique opportunity for researchers and academics to collaborate with a United Nations agency addressing global development challenges.

By participating in the program, fellows can:

Gain valuable experience working with a leading international organization.

Contribute to projects focused on global food security and sustainable agriculture.

Expand their professional networks within international development institutions.

Strengthen their research, analytical, and policy development skills.

Enhance their academic and professional careers in fields related to agriculture, development, and environmental sustainability.

For individuals interested in working at the intersection of research, policy, and global development, this fellowship offers an exceptional platform to contribute meaningfully to international efforts to end hunger and transform food systems.

Application Process

Interested applicants must submit their applications through the Official FAO recruitment portal.

Applicants are required to complete their online profile, upload necessary academic documents, and provide a research proposal outlining how their expertise aligns with FAO’s work and objectives.

Only applications submitted through the FAO recruitment system will be considered for evaluation.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the FAO Fellows Programme 2026 is 25 August 2026 at 11:59 PM.

Candidates are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to ensure all required documents are properly uploaded and reviewed.

Click HERE to learn more about the FAO Fellows Programme 2026.

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post FAO Fellows Programme 2026: Join the United Nations to Advance Global Food Security and Sustainable Agriculture appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OPEC Fund Young Professional Development Program 2026: A Prestigious Two-Year Global Career Opportunity in International Development
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications are now open for the OPEC Fund Young Professional Development Program (YPDP) 2026, a highly competitive two-year professional development initiative designed to prepare the next generation of global development leaders. The Young Professional Development Program is an international career development initiative offered by the OPEC Fund for International Development, a leading multilateral development finance […]

The post OPEC Fund Young Professional Development Program 2026: A Prestigious Two-Year Global Career Opportunity in International Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the OPEC Fund Young Professional Development Program (YPDP) 2026, a highly competitive two-year professional development initiative designed to prepare the next generation of global development leaders.

The Young Professional Development Program is an international career development initiative offered by the OPEC Fund for International Development, a leading multilateral development finance institution headquartered in Vienna, Austria. The program provides talented young professionals from OPEC Fund member countries with an opportunity to gain professional training, international exposure, and hands-on experience in development finance and global development policy.

This prestigious program is designed for individuals who are passionate about solving global challenges and contributing to sustainable economic and social development. Participants will receive structured professional training, mentorship, departmental rotations, and direct exposure to the operational work of a global development institution.

Applications for the 2026 cohort opened on 4 March 2026, and the deadline to apply is 11 April 2026.

The Young Professional Development Program represents a unique pathway for early-career professionals seeking to build long-term careers in international development, global policy, development finance, and sustainable development initiatives.

About the OPEC Fund for International Development

The OPEC Fund for International Development is a multilateral development finance institution established by member countries of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC). The institution’s mission is to promote economic development and reduce poverty in developing countries around the world.

Through financing, partnerships, and technical assistance, the OPEC Fund supports projects that strengthen infrastructure, improve social services, expand economic opportunities, and enhance global development cooperation.

The organization works closely with governments, international organizations, financial institutions, and development partners to support sustainable growth and improve the quality of life for millions of people.

Its development work focuses on critical sectors including:

  • Infrastructure development

  • Energy access

  • Water and sanitation

  • Education

  • Healthcare

  • Food security

  • Employment and private sector development

  • Support for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)

The Young Professional Development Program reflects the OPEC Fund’s commitment to investing in emerging talent and preparing future leaders who can contribute to global development efforts.

Overview of the Young Professional Development Program (YPDP)

The Young Professional Development Program (YPDP) is a structured two-year program designed to equip young professionals with the knowledge, skills, and practical experience required to build careers in international development.

The program focuses on preparing participants to understand and contribute to the strategic and operational work of the OPEC Fund.

During the two-year program, participants will work across various departments and units within the organization, allowing them to gain insight into the different functions that contribute to the institution’s overall development mission.

Through a combination of professional training, mentoring, coaching, and practical work experience, the program enables participants to develop a strong understanding of development finance and international cooperation.

The program also aims to help young professionals appreciate how different teams and departments collaborate to achieve the OPEC Fund’s broader vision of promoting sustainable development globally.

Participants will also gain valuable exposure to international development projects and policies that address pressing global challenges such as poverty reduction, economic growth, infrastructure development, and environmental sustainability.

Key Benefits of the Program

Participants selected for the Young Professional Development Program benefit from a comprehensive professional development experience designed to strengthen both technical expertise and leadership capacity.

Structured Professional Training

Participants will receive specialized training designed to deepen their knowledge of development finance, project management, policy analysis, and international cooperation.

These training opportunities help participants understand how development institutions operate and how financial instruments are used to support economic growth and poverty reduction.

Departmental Rotations

A central feature of the program is the opportunity for participants to rotate across different departments and operational units within the OPEC Fund.

These rotations provide participants with exposure to a variety of functional areas, helping them understand the strategic and operational roles played by different teams within the organization.

This experience enables participants to build a holistic understanding of development operations.

Mentorship and Coaching

Participants will benefit from mentorship and coaching provided by experienced professionals and senior staff members within the OPEC Fund.

Mentorship provides guidance on career development, leadership skills, and professional growth in international development institutions.

Participants receive valuable insights into how global development organizations operate and how professionals contribute to policy and development initiatives.

On-the-Job Learning Experience

Participants will engage in real development projects and initiatives, gaining hands-on experience while working alongside experienced professionals.

This practical exposure enables participants to apply their academic knowledge to real-world challenges and develop problem-solving skills relevant to global development work.

Access to Professional Networks

Participants will have opportunities to build connections with development professionals, policy experts, and institutional partners.

These networking opportunities are valuable for building long-term careers in international development and global policy institutions.

Career Opportunities After Program Completion

One of the most attractive aspects of the Young Professional Development Program is the potential career pathway it offers.

Upon successful completion of the two-year program, participants may be offered an entry-level professional position at the OPEC Fund, depending on their performance and the organization’s staffing needs.

This opportunity allows participants to transition directly into professional roles where they can contribute to international development projects that support economic growth and social progress.

Participants who receive job offers are expected to commit to serving the OPEC Fund for at least two additional years after completing the program.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants interested in the Young Professional Development Program must meet several eligibility requirements.

Applicants must:

  • Be nationals of an OPEC Fund member country

  • Be 30 years old or younger at the time of application

  • Hold at least a Master’s degree from a reputable university

  • Demonstrate excellent academic performance

  • Be fluent in English

  • Possess a minimum of three years of professional work experience

Knowledge of additional languages may be considered an advantage.

Applicants must also demonstrate the ability to work in a multicultural and international environment, as the OPEC Fund collaborates with global partners across multiple regions.

Candidates should also demonstrate a strong understanding of the OPEC Fund’s development mandate and its role in promoting sustainable development worldwide.

Relevant Fields of Study

Applicants are expected to specialize in academic or professional fields relevant to the operations of the OPEC Fund.

Relevant disciplines include:

  • Engineering

  • Economics

  • Finance

  • Business Administration

  • Information Technology

  • Law

  • Human Resources

  • Other related fields connected to international development and cooperation

These disciplines align with the technical and operational work carried out by the OPEC Fund in its global development initiatives.

Application Process

Interested candidates must submit their applications through the Official Online Application Portal.

Applicants are required to submit the following documents:

  • A completed YPDP online application form

  • A Curriculum Vitae (CV)

  • An application essay

  • Academic certificates and transcripts

Applicants must ensure that their application essay and academic transcripts are uploaded in the “Additional Documents” section of the application form.

Applicants should also provide a valid and active email address to receive updates regarding the selection process.

It is important to ensure that all questions in the application form are completed accurately and that all required documents are submitted before the deadline.

Application Essay Requirements

Applicants must submit an original essay of fewer than 1,000 words addressing two key themes:

  1. The potential role of development institutions such as the OPEC Fund in helping developing countries overcome social, environmental, and economic challenges.

  2. The applicant’s vision of how they would contribute to international development if selected to work at the OPEC Fund.

Applicants may focus their essay on a particular region, development challenge, or area of expertise.

The essay should demonstrate strong analytical thinking, a deep understanding of global development challenges, and a commitment to sustainable development.

Core Competencies

The OPEC Fund seeks candidates who demonstrate strong professional competencies required for working in international development institutions.

Key competencies include:

Collaboration and Teamwork

The ability to work effectively with colleagues, partners, and stakeholders to achieve shared development goals.

Communication

The ability to communicate ideas clearly and effectively to diverse audiences.

Cross-Cultural Sensitivity

The ability to work respectfully and effectively in multicultural environments and appreciate diverse perspectives.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the OPEC Fund Young Professional Development Program 2026 is 11 April 2026.

Given the competitive nature of the program, interested applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their application materials early and submit their applications well before the deadline.

Only candidates who meet the eligibility requirements and submit complete applications will be considered for the program.

Shortlisted candidates will be contacted following the review process.

Click HERE to learn more about the OPEC Fund Young Professional Development Program

Final Thoughts

The OPEC Fund Young Professional Development Program offers an exceptional opportunity for talented young professionals who aspire to build careers in international development and global policy.

Through structured training, mentorship, and hands-on experience, the program prepares participants to become effective development professionals capable of addressing complex global challenges.

For individuals passionate about sustainable development, economic growth, and international cooperation, this program represents a powerful stepping stone toward a meaningful career in global development.

Interested candidates should review the Eligibility Criteria carefully and submit their applications before the deadline.

Visit OFY for more fellowship opportunities.

The post OPEC Fund Young Professional Development Program 2026: A Prestigious Two-Year Global Career Opportunity in International Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PhD Position on Older Migrants’ Family Networks at the University of Groningen
item.dc:creator: Ankita Baidya
item.description:
The University of Groningen in the Netherlands is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD position titled “Mapping Older Migrants’ Family Networks.” The position offers an opportunity to conduct interdisciplinary research on ageing, migration, and social cohesion while working with large-scale population data. The project will explore how family networks among older migrants influence well-being, […]

The post PhD Position on Older Migrants’ Family Networks at the University of Groningen appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Groningen in the Netherlands is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD position titled “Mapping Older Migrants’ Family Networks.” The position offers an opportunity to conduct interdisciplinary research on ageing, migration, and social cohesion while working with large-scale population data. The project will explore how family networks among older migrants influence well-being, loneliness, and social integration in the Netherlands.

Application Deadline: 1 April 2026 (23:59 Dutch local time).

This four-year PhD project is hosted by the Faculty of Behavioural and Social Sciences at the University of Groningen and is part of the SOCION research consortium. The position is offered on a full-time basis (1.0 FTE) with a monthly salary ranging from €3,059 to €3,881, depending on the stage of the PhD programme.

Research Focus in University of Groningen

The project focuses on understanding the structure and dynamics of family networks among migrants aged 50 and above in the Netherlands. Family relationships often play a crucial role in providing emotional support, caregiving, and social stability in later life. However, migrants may experience different forms of family structures compared to non-migrants due to transnational ties, geographical dispersion of relatives, and institutional factors.

Using microdata from Statistics Netherlands (CBS), the research will analyse differences in the size, composition, and spatial distribution of kinship networks among older migrants and non-migrants. The project aims to identify how these family networks relate to outcomes such as health, well-being, and experiences of loneliness.

Another important dimension of the project is the comparison of various migrant groups in the Netherlands. By examining migrants with different migration histories and backgrounds, the research will explore how social policies, integration frameworks, and migration pathways shape family structures and support systems in later life.

Research Design and Methods

The PhD project will primarily use a mixed-methods approach that combines quantitative and qualitative research methods.

The quantitative component will rely on CBS microdata, which includes detailed demographic, migration, and household information. This dataset will allow the researcher to construct typologies of family networks by analysing factors such as network size, intergenerational ties, and geographical proximity between relatives.

Geospatial mapping will also be used to examine the spatial distribution of family members and to identify regional differences in family network structures. This analysis will help determine whether the location of family members affects the social support available to older migrants.

If the candidate’s expertise allows, the project may also include a qualitative component. This could involve interviews with older migrants to understand how they perceive and navigate their family networks and social relationships. Such insights would complement the statistical analysis and provide a deeper understanding of lived experiences.

Contribution to Social Cohesion Research

The research forms part of the SOCION consortium, which examines how social cohesion is shaped in modern societies. Older migrants represent a particularly important group in discussions of social cohesion because they often maintain family connections across borders while also interacting with local welfare systems and communities.

By mapping and comparing family networks of migrants and non-migrants, the project will provide new insights into how social ties contribute to social cohesion. The findings are expected to support policymakers and institutions in identifying vulnerable groups and designing more effective social support strategies.

Candidate Profile

Applicants should hold a Master’s degree in sociology or a closely related discipline. Candidates should have a strong interest in research on migration, social cohesion, or ageing. A solid methodological background is required, ideally including experience with both quantitative and qualitative research methods.

Because the project involves extensive data analysis, applicants must have prior experience with programming or statistical analysis. Knowledge of Python is considered an advantage, although it can also be developed during the PhD programme. Professional proficiency in English is required, while knowledge of Dutch is considered a plus.

The university is looking for candidates who are collaborative, motivated, and interested in interdisciplinary research. Strong analytical skills, planning abilities, and communication skills are also important for this role.

Employment Conditions

The selected candidate will receive a four-year employment contract, initially offered for one year and extended for the remaining three years after a positive evaluation. The position includes 232 vacation hours per year based on a 38-hour work week, along with an end-of-year bonus and holiday allowance.

The University of Groningen also offers extensive opportunities for professional and personal development, making it an attractive environment for early-career researchers.

About the University of Groningen

The University of Groningen is one of the leading research universities in Europe. It is known for its strong emphasis on interdisciplinary research and collaboration with societal partners, including businesses, government institutions, and civic organisations.

The Faculty of Behavioural and Social Sciences hosts more than 4,500 students and approximately 650 staff members who work on major social challenges related to health, education, social inclusion, and sustainability. The faculty focuses on strengthening both individual and societal resilience through innovative research and teaching.

This PhD position offers a unique opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge research on migration, ageing, and social networks while working in an internationally recognised academic environment.

Application can be accessed from here.

To know more about such opportunities, click here.

The post PhD Position on Older Migrants’ Family Networks at the University of Groningen appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EIT Food Call for Expression of Interest: Evaluators in Agriculture 2026
item.dc:creator: Ankita Baidya
item.description:
EIT Food has opened a call for experts in the agriculture sector to join its database of independent evaluators. This opportunity invites experienced professionals to contribute to the evaluation of innovative projects that aim to transform the global food system. Selected experts will help assess proposals submitted to EIT Food’s various funding and entrepreneurship programmes. […]

The post EIT Food Call for Expression of Interest: Evaluators in Agriculture 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
EIT Food has opened a call for experts in the agriculture sector to join its database of independent evaluators. This opportunity invites experienced professionals to contribute to the evaluation of innovative projects that aim to transform the global food system. Selected experts will help assess proposals submitted to EIT Food’s various funding and entrepreneurship programmes.

Deadline: 29 March 2026 (23:59 CET)

EIT Food is the world’s largest food innovation community and is supported by the European Union. Its mission is to create a healthier, more sustainable, and resilient food system by investing in innovation, entrepreneurship, education, and collaborative projects. Through open calls and competitive programmes, EIT Food funds initiatives that promote healthier diets, reduce environmental impact, and increase transparency across the food value chain.

To ensure the highest quality of funded activities, EIT Food relies on a network of independent evaluators with strong technical and professional expertise. These evaluators play a key role in assessing proposals and helping identify the most promising innovations and projects.

Role of Evaluators for EIT Food

Experts selected for the evaluator database will support EIT Food in reviewing proposals submitted to its calls for projects and programmes. Their responsibilities may include participating in online training sessions, reviewing full project proposals, and assessing the feasibility, financial structure, and innovation potential of applications.

Evaluators are also expected to provide qualitative assessments based on predefined evaluation criteria. They may be asked to prepare written evaluation reports, proofread documentation, and contribute to quality assurance. In addition, they will participate in panel meetings with other experts where funding recommendations are discussed and finalized.

Through this process, evaluators help determine which projects receive funding and support from EIT Food.

Areas of Expertise

The current call focuses on increasing the pool of experts in agriculture-related fields. Applicants should have substantial experience in at least one relevant area within the agrifood system.

Key areas include regenerative and climate-smart agriculture, soil health and carbon sequestration, biodiversity and ecosystem services, sustainable farming practices, and crop or livestock management. Expertise in sustainable inputs, nutrient management, pest and disease management, and water management systems is also highly valued.

EIT Food is also seeking professionals working in digital and data-driven agriculture, including artificial intelligence, remote sensing, GIS systems, and agricultural data management. Experts in aquaculture, diversified production systems, and farm systems design are also encouraged to apply.

Additional areas of interest include sustainable value chains, traceability, market access, regenerative finance, impact investment, and carbon accounting systems used in agriculture. Experience in policy development, sustainability certification, stakeholder engagement, or circular bioeconomy innovation may also be relevant.

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, candidates must have strong professional expertise in at least one of the specified areas. In addition, applicants must demonstrate fluency in English, both written and spoken, as this is the working language for the evaluation process.

Applicants should have the ability to collaborate effectively with other evaluators and contribute constructively to panel discussions. Strong analytical skills and a critical but open approach to reviewing proposals are essential.

EIT Food also aims to maintain balanced and diverse evaluation panels. As part of this effort, the organization targets at least 40 percent representation of the under-represented gender in its evaluator groups.

Application Process

Individuals interested in joining the evaluator database must complete an online application form and upload their CV, providing detailed information about their professional experience and expertise.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and some applicants may be invited to participate in a mock evaluation as part of the selection process. Candidates will be assessed based on technical expertise, previous experience evaluating EU-funded projects, and familiarity with the EIT ecosystem.

Successful applicants will be notified by 30 April 2026 and added to the EIT Food evaluator database.

Compensation and Assignments

Being included in the evaluator database does not automatically guarantee assignments. However, when evaluators are selected to review proposals for a specific call, they will receive compensation for their work.

EIT Food pays evaluators €56.25 per hour, with an estimated 2–7 hours required to evaluate a single proposal. Evaluators are typically assigned more than one proposal, and the specific conditions and timelines for each assignment are communicated in advance.

All evaluation work must be completed according to the agreed timelines and quality standards set by EIT Food.

Contributing to Food System Innovation

By joining the evaluator database, experts gain the opportunity to contribute to shaping the future of the agrifood sector. Evaluators play a vital role in identifying high-impact projects that advance sustainability, innovation, and resilience within global food systems.

Professionals with deep knowledge of agriculture, food innovation, and sustainability are encouraged to apply and become part of this international network of experts.

Application can be accessed from here.

To know more about such opportunities, click here.

The post EIT Food Call for Expression of Interest: Evaluators in Agriculture 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Fully Funded PhD in Education at Uppsala University, Sweden – 2 Positions Available
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Are you passionate about advancing research in education and shaping the future of learning and educational policy? The Uppsala University in Uppsala, Sweden is inviting applications for two fully funded PhD positions in Education at its Department of Education. The positions are expected to begin on 1 September 2026 and will run for four years […]

The post Apply Now: Fully Funded PhD in Education at Uppsala University, Sweden – 2 Positions Available appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about advancing research in education and shaping the future of learning and educational policy? The Uppsala University in Uppsala, Sweden is inviting applications for two fully funded PhD positions in Education at its Department of Education. The positions are expected to begin on 1 September 2026 and will run for four years of full-time doctoral study.

This opportunity allows doctoral candidates to work within dynamic research groups, contribute to high-quality academic research, and complete a PhD dissertation in Education at one of Europe’s leading research universities.

About the Department of Education

The Uppsala University Department of Education (EDU) offers research and academic programs at undergraduate, master’s, and doctoral levels. The department conducts research in areas such as:

  • Curriculum Studies

  • Education

  • Sociology of Education

With around 250 staff members, including 175 teachers and researchers, 45 doctoral students, and 30 administrators, the department maintains a strong research environment dedicated to understanding how education shapes individuals and societies.

The field of Education explores how people—children, youth, and adults—learn and develop in different social, cultural, and institutional contexts such as schools, families, workplaces, and higher education institutions. Researchers examine how knowledge, values, and skills are created, maintained, and transformed across generations.

PhD Research Areas

Successful applicants will join one of the department’s research groups, each focusing on different aspects of education and learning.

1. Studies in Educational Policy and Educational Philosophy (STEP)

This group explores educational governance, policy, and professional practices. Research focuses on how knowledge, values, and identities are shaped through educational systems, particularly in relation to globalization, migration, digitalization, and the knowledge-based society.

2. Research on Educational Leadership (REL)

REL examines the relationship between education, leadership, and learning. The interdisciplinary group studies leadership practices and the conditions that support or limit leadership in educational contexts.

3. Childhood, Learning and Identities as Interactional Practices (CLIP)

CLIP focuses on children’s and young people’s social interactions, identities, and learning processes in schools and everyday life. Research themes include multilingualism, digital practices, mobility, and social relations.

4. Child and Youth Studies (BUV)

This group studies identity and socialization among children, youth, and young adults across different social environments. The research critically explores inequalities and structural conditions affecting young people.

5. Pedagogics and Special Needs (PS)

The PS group focuses on inclusive education and support systems, particularly in schools and preschools. Research includes theoretical analyses, empirical studies, and interventions aimed at improving learning environments.

Why Study at Uppsala University?

Founded in 1477, Uppsala University is one of Europe’s most prestigious institutions, with over 53,000 students and 7,600 staff members. The university is internationally recognized for its commitment to high-quality education, groundbreaking research, and societal impact.

Living in Sweden also offers a strong work-life balance, world-class research infrastructure, and a vibrant international academic community.

PhD Responsibilities

The doctoral position is a full-time temporary employment for four years leading to a PhD dissertation. As a doctoral candidate, you will:

  • Conduct independent research and write a doctoral thesis

  • Participate actively in departmental seminars and academic activities

  • Complete required doctoral coursework

  • Contribute to teaching or administrative duties (up to 20%), which may extend the employment period beyond four years

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the general requirements for doctoral studies at Uppsala University. To be eligible, candidates must have:

  • An advanced degree (Master’s level or equivalent)

  • At least 240 ECTS credits, including 60 credits at advanced level

  • At least 90 credits in Education or an equivalent relevant field

  • Excellent written and spoken English

Knowledge of Swedish is not required at the time of application, but admitted candidates are expected to learn Swedish during the program.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following documents:

  • Completed and signed PhD application form

  • Copies of Bachelor’s and Master’s theses (or equivalent)

  • A short CV including publications (if any)

  • Degree certificates and academic transcripts

  • A 4–5 page research proposal outlining:

    • Research questions

    • Theoretical and disciplinary context

    • Relevant previous research

    • Data sources and methodology

    • Relevance of the proposed thesis project

    • Motivation for pursuing doctoral studies at Uppsala University

All application documents must be submitted in English or Swedish. Documents in other languages must be officially translated into English.

Employment Details

  • Position type: Temporary doctoral position

  • Number of positions: 2

  • Employment type: Full-time (100%)

  • Start date: 1 September 2026 (or as agreed)

  • Location: Uppsala, Sweden

  • Salary: Fixed salary according to PhD student agreements

  • Employment period: Up to four years of full-time doctoral studies

  • Reference number: UFV-PA 2026/400

The position may require security vetting, and candidates must pass the vetting process if applicable.

Application Deadline

2 April 2026

Applications must be submitted through the official recruitment system of Uppsala University.

For more information about this position, click here. 

Discover more PhD Opportunities Here.

The post Apply Now: Fully Funded PhD in Education at Uppsala University, Sweden – 2 Positions Available appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
